message:
feat(frame): add na_position control, ignore_index concat, fillna_dict, replace_dict, and groupby agg_multi

Sorting with na_position parameter:
- Add compare_scalars_with_na_position() helper that accepts a na_first
  flag to place missing values (Null/NaN) at the beginning or end of
  sorted output. The existing compare_scalars_with_na_last() now
  delegates to this new function with na_first=false.
- Add Series::sort_values_na(ascending, na_position) matching pandas
  s.sort_values(ascending=..., na_position='first'|'last').
- Add DataFrame::sort_values_na(column, ascending, na_position) matching
  pandas df.sort_values(by=col, ascending=..., na_position=...).
- Existing sort_values() methods remain unchanged as thin wrappers
  defaulting to na_position='last'.

Concatenation with ignore_index:
- Add concat_series_with_ignore_index() and
  concat_dataframes_with_ignore_index() matching pandas
  pd.concat([...], ignore_index=True/False). When ignore_index is true
  the result gets a fresh 0..n-1 integer index instead of preserving
  original labels.
- Existing concat_series() and concat_dataframes() delegate with
  ignore_index=false for backward compatibility.

Per-column fillna:
- Add DataFrame::fillna_dict() matching pandas df.fillna({'col': val}).
  Columns not in the map are left unchanged; referencing a missing column
  is an error.

Per-column replace:
- Add DataFrame::replace_dict() matching pandas
  df.replace({'col': {old: new}}). Uses Scalar::semantic_eq for
  matching so NaN values can be targeted. Columns not in the map are
  left untouched; referencing a missing column is an error.

Groupby multi-function aggregation:
- Add DataFrameGroupBy::agg_multi() matching pandas
  df.groupby(col).agg({'A': ['sum','mean'], 'B': ['count']}).
  Output columns are named {col}_{func}. Attempting to aggregate the
  group-by key column is rejected with an error.

All new methods include comprehensive unit tests covering happy paths,
edge cases, and error conditions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
